mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower equity discount rate, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is valid when the policy rate is moving down, and it fails if rate cuts occur during acute recessionary stress that overwhelms valuation support.
failure_condition: If 20d forward SPX returns are not positive on average after sustained FEDFUNDS declines, or if the sign flips during easing episodes in more than half of rolling 3-year windows, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is falling while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real rates rise only weakly and policy restraint begins to ease in expected terms, supporting equity multiples over the next week. The mechanism is falsified if inflation decelerates but SPX weakens because the market interprets the move as demand collapse rather than benign disinflation.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation drops below its prior 6-month average while FEDFUNDS stays above 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases do not produce positive 5d SPX returns more often than 50%, or if the relationship disappears when FEDFUNDS is above 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent low indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which should pressure risk assets over the next month. The mechanism fails when unemployment rises alongside other strong demand indicators and equity investors ignore the labor signal.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If 20d forward SPX returns are positive in a majority of unemployment-shock episodes, or if the signal has no negative effect after controlling for the prior 6-month level of UNRATE, reject it.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broadening domestic demand, improving revenue expectations for listed firms and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The claim fails if retail sales improve but housing weakens enough to signal only inventory restocking rather than durable demand.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST improvement does not outperform each series alone for predicting 60d SPX returns, or if the combined signal is negative in more than 50% of expansion windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO after a prior trough reflects industrial reacceleration, stronger cyclical earnings, and tighter production-to-demand balance, which should lift equities over the next month. The hypothesis fails if industrial output rises only because of temporary inventory rebuilding without accompanying broad market gains.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after at least 2 consecutive negative months
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO inflections are not followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than 55%, or if the average return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive while PAYEMS is also expanding, the economy is generating both output and payroll income, which supports earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is falsified if GDP and payroll gains coexist but markets sell off because the expansion is already fully priced.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth is positive and PAYEMS 3-month average change is above zero
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-payroll condition fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of samples, or if the combined effect is weaker than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising unemployment regime, a simultaneous decline in PAYEMS confirms genuine labor-market deterioration rather than a temporary survey artifact, creating negative equity returns over the next week. The mechanism fails if payroll weakness appears without unemployment deterioration and equities stay resilient.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage points in 2 months and PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not lead to negative 5d SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the joint condition adds no predictive value beyond UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a still-rising CPIAUCSL combined with FEDFUNDS above 2.0% implies the Fed is behind inflation, so discount rates stay pressured and S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next month. The signal fails once inflation has clearly rolled over or policy becomes explicitly accommodative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-accelerating-inflation periods do not produce negative 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the sign reverses once inflation accelerates.
